name: Easy | Topcoder Skill Builder Competition | ReactJS | Getting Started
description:
## Getting Started

This is the **EASY 250 Points Competition.**

Your task in **this** challenge is to understand what problem we are trying to solve and build a prototype structure to be used in the Medium and hard challenges. 

**You are required to use ReactJS as the frontend technology. You may use any Backend of your choice. **If you are not familiar with the backend, you can use Backend as a Service technologies like Firebase or Supabase (both are free to use).

Note: You are only required to build a subset of the application in this challenge. The rest will be covered in Medium and Hard challenges.


## What do you need to do in this Easy challenge?

In this easy challenge, the requirements from you are as follows. You need to design and build the functionality:

**Client Signup:**

This is where a client will be able to sign up with the following details: Name, Email, and Password.

**Client Login:**

This is where a client will be able to login with the following details: Email, and Password.

**Navbar:**

The navbar navigation will have two options: 1) Profile, and 2) Create Learning Space** **

**Client profile:**

When the client clicks on their profile from the navigation, a screen showing all the details of them should render. The required profile details are: Name, Biography and Country of residence, interests (ReactJS tags) and a placeholder photo thumbnail.


## Recommended learning material

[React Authentication Tutorial ��� How to Set Up Auth with Firebase V9 and React Router V6](https://www.freecodecamp.org/news/react-firebase-authentication-and-crud-operations/)

[User Registration With Firebase and React | CSS-Tricks](https://css-tricks.com/user-registration-authentication-firebase-react/)

[react-tagsinput - npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/react-tagsinput)


## Submission Deliverables

You must follow this submission folder structure for your scoring:



   * Create a folder with ���easy��� as the folder name then zip. 
   * You are required to take screenshots of all the screens you built in this challenge and place it in a folder called ���screenshots��� within the ���easy��� folder
   * Zip that ���easy��� folder and submit to the challenge.


## Topcoder Skill Builder Competition | ReactJS | Problems

Host Challenge Linkt: https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/a4826c63-5e86-45f7-899b-6ed2a3aa60f4

Easy: 250 Points. Challenge Link: https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/a47ab6c2-23fa-47d3-862d-f4c725245bea

Medium: 500 Points. Challenge Link: https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/c686d72a-f0da-4e30-9eca-b75f317e5931

Hard: 1000 Points. Challenge Link: https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/305e0763-dd63-4883-9133-55504503953d

name: Topcoder Skill Builder Competition | ReactJS
description:
Welcome to our Topcoder Skill Builder "**ReactJS**" Competition! 

We are excited to kick-off our plans to help everyone skill up, have fun, and earn more money through Topcoder Gigs and Competitions. Jump-in, build your skills and as always let us know what you think and where Topcoder can continue to improve and bring competitions that are meaningful to you.

[ReactJS](https://reactjs.org/) (also known as React.js or React) is a free and open-source front-end JavaScript library for building user interfaces based on UI components. 

In this ReactJS Skill Builder Competition you will learn in depth what is ReactJS ,and build an awesome co-learning application that can be used by you, and other people to learn something great together. The content of this skill builder will not just help you understand the ReactJS, but also have a working ReactJS application that would prove your expertise in this domain. You will also encounter a lot of learning material in this Skill builder.


## Topcoder Skill Builder Competition Structure



* Skill Builder Competition will have three (3) problems (Easy, Medium, and Hard) to solve. 
* **IMPORTANT:** This Skill Builder Competition is intended for people seeking gig opportunities After the competition we will be adding you to the TaaS Pool of ReactJS Developers, where you would be able to be assigned to available gigs.


## Problem Statement

Co-Learn is a platform for people who get a thrill from learning and love to share a knowledge. The purpose of Co-learn is to create an environment for learning for the sake of learning.

In this Skill builder series, we will build an awesome colearning application. Although most of the focus will be on the frontend, you will be required to do a little of backend integration for the functionality to work. 


## Final Submission Guidelines

**What to Submit**

**You are required to submit on the Host challenge (this challenge) and on the sub-challenges (Easy, Medium, and Hard).**

On this Challenge, please submit a .txt file using the ���Submit��� button before the submission phase ends. In this file include:



* Your Topcoder handle (The one displayed in the top right corner near the Profile picture)
* ALL THE SUBMISSIONS WITHOUT ABOVE INFORMATION WILL BE REJECTED AND WON���T BE PAID. IMPORTANT: Submit the above details before the Submission Phase ends.


## Judging Criteria



* Frontend requirement implementation 50%
* User Experience 15%
* Rendering Performance 10%
* Code quality and code size 10% (using libraries like [Store | Redux](https://redux.js.org/api/store) will count positive) 
* Ease of deployment 10%
* Backend implementation 5% 


## Instructions



* We have prepared three problems: Easy, Medium, and Hard, which are worth 250, 500, 1000 points respectively. Points are based on the difficulty of the problem.
* The competitions are related to each other.
* The links to the problems/competitions are provided below.
* Each problem has some detailed requirements to achieve. We will judge your submission based on the criteria mentioned above.
* If there is a tie, the submission time will be taken into account.

[Here is the leaderboard with individual problem scores](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1fWykiymzRLVJ_gLefMnLi6G8wK04TK9-rb8w-jYicoo/edit?usp=sharing). (Updated at the end of the Skill builder)


## Problems

Host Challenge Linkt: https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/a4826c63-5e86-45f7-899b-6ed2a3aa60f4

Easy: 250 Points. Challenge Link: https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/a47ab6c2-23fa-47d3-862d-f4c725245bea

Medium: 500 Points. Challenge Link: https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/c686d72a-f0da-4e30-9eca-b75f317e5931

Hard: 1000 Points. Challenge Link: https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/305e0763-dd63-4883-9133-55504503953d

name: Hard | Topcoder Skill Builder Competition | ReactJS  | Annotations
description:
This is the **HARD 1000 Points Competition**

By now you have built the base of the application. It's time to add more and complete the existing functionality \
 \
 \
**You are required to use ReactJS as the frontend technology. You may use any Backend of your choice. **If you are not familiar with the backend, you can use Backend as a Service technologies like Firebase or Supabase (both are free to use).


## What do you need to do in this Hard challenge?

In the application so far, we have built screens for customer authentication, homepage, learning spaces, and profiles. Now we will build some more screens.

**Creating a Learning Space**



* An authenticated user should be able to create a new learning space from the Navbar menu title ***Create Space***
* A new space creation screen should be rendered.
* The screen should have a form with the following fields: Title, description, prerequisites (ReactJS tags), thumbnail.

**Creating a Post**



* Within a learning space, the user should be able to create a new post.
* The post will have a title and a body. The body should use a Markdown text-editor input. You can refer to the reference to understand more about markdown editors.
* When viewing a post, the post can be saved locally as a PDF

**Creating a Comment**



* Within a learning space Post, the user should be able to add a new comment.
* The comment will have a body. The body should use a Markdown text-editor input. You can refer to the reference to understand more about markdown editors.

**Upvotes/Downvotes**



* The users should be able to upvote/downvote a Post, or a comment.

**Annotation**



* Annotation refers to a note by way of explanation or comment added to a text or diagram.
* Within a learning space, inside a post, a user can highlight a text, on highlight it will display an icon to annotate that text. On clicking the icon, a pop up should appear with two items. The first being the highlighted text, and the second being a text field where user can enter their thoughts about the highlighted text.  
* The user should be able to see the text highlighted and their annotation on hovering on it after save. 


## Recommended learning material

[Markdown Editor for React.](https://uiwjs.github.io/react-md-editor/)

[Generating a PDF file from React Components - Stack Overflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/44989119/generating-a-pdf-file-from-react-components)

[Annotation Example.gif](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1AfNytnWfkRK5hr34LIXSMxCWU7AMUIc5/view?usp=share_link)

[Text Annotation And Highlighting Library - Annotate.js | CSS Script](https://www.cssscript.com/text-annotation-highlighting/)

[Medium-like text highlighting in React | by Pargles Dall'Oglio | JavaScript in Plain English](https://javascript.plainenglish.io/medium-like-text-highlighting-in-react-afa35a29a81a)


## Submission Deliverables

You must follow this submission folder structure for your scoring:

   * Create a folder with ***hard*** as the folder name then zip. 
   * You are required to screen record a video of all the functionality you built in this challenge. Upload the video on your google drive and share the link in a text file that you will place in the ***hard*** folder.
   * Zip that ***hard*** folder and submit to the challenge.


## Topcoder Skill Builder Competition | ReactJS | Problems

Host Challenge Linkt: https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/a4826c63-5e86-45f7-899b-6ed2a3aa60f4

Easy: 250 Points. Challenge Link: https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/a47ab6c2-23fa-47d3-862d-f4c725245bea

Medium: 500 Points. Challenge Link: https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/c686d72a-f0da-4e30-9eca-b75f317e5931

Hard: 1000 Points. Challenge Link: https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/305e0763-dd63-4883-9133-55504503953d

name: Medium | Topcoder Skill Builder Competition | ReactJS | Co-learning
description:
This is the **MEDIUM 500 Points Competition** 

By now you have built the base of the application. Its time to add more and complete the existing functionality \


**Important points before you begin:**

**You are required to use ReactJS as the frontend technology. You may use any Backend of your choice. **If you are not familiar with the backend, you can use Backend as a Service technologies like Firebase or Supabase (both are free to use).

**You are only required to build a subset of the application in this challenge. The rest will be covered in the Hard challenge.**


## What do you need to do in this Medium challenge?

In the easy challenge, you designed the initial screens, we will now move forward and add more interesting screens:

**Homescreen**

This screen will be visible to both authenticated and non-authenticated users.

The Homescreen will look like this:



* The first section will be a centered heading. If the user is not signed, it will say Sign up to start learning If the User is signed it, it will say Join a learning space below
* Below this section will be the following Co-learning Spaces section.
* There will be a grid of ReactJS cards component displaying all co-learning spaces. Each card will display a thumbnail of a co-learning space, the title, number of members and last update in that space. There is no specific design orientation, you are free to design how it looks, but it has to be in a card and with the information mentioned above. More information about card component is mentioned in the reference section.

**Viewing Learning Space**



* When clicked on any co-learning card from the homepage, the app should render that co-learning space screen.
* A learning space shall display an overview of the learning space. 
* A learning space shall display a button to join/leave a learning space near its title. If the user is not authenticated, it should display Sign up to Join otherwise Join
* A learning space shall display a prerequisites list, demonstrating the learning requirements for the learning space. We will cover how this list will appear in the HARD challenge.
* A learning space shall display a list of Posts created inside the learning space in a sorted order by date.
* A learning space shall display a button to create a Post.
* A learning space shall display a list of active users that are currently visiting the learning space. Clicking on one of the users will reroute to their profile page.
* A learning space shall display a list of related learning spaces, which are determined by the keywords used during the creation of learning spaces. We will cover how the keywords are listed in the HARD challenge.


## Recommended learning material

[Cards - React-Bootstrap Documentation](https://react-bootstrap.github.io/components/cards/)

[react-ui-cards - npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/react-ui-cards)


## Submission Deliverables

You must follow this submission folder structure for your scoring:



   * Create a folder with medium as the folder name then zip. 
   * You are required to screen record a video of all the functionality you built in this challenge. Upload the video on your google drive and share the link in a text file that you will place in the medium folder.
   * Zip that medium folder and submit to the challenge.


## Topcoder Skill Builder Competition | ReactJS | Problems

Host Challenge Linkt: https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/a4826c63-5e86-45f7-899b-6ed2a3aa60f4

Easy: 250 Points. Challenge Link: https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/a47ab6c2-23fa-47d3-862d-f4c725245bea

Medium: 500 Points. Challenge Link: https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/c686d72a-f0da-4e30-9eca-b75f317e5931

name: Topcoder O/R F2F - Save for later button submits the review score
description:
#### Design Assets & Downloadable
* Figma contains downloadable assets and style guidance:  https://www.figma.com/file/WDmII42s0WSC3PMyKohrot/Payments-Track-Reskin?node-id=4157%3A11532

https://docs.google.com/document/d/15Bk8daay8FDAsKeWPhv4fUx6eMtMq5Jn9aezlVNqfqw/edit?usp=sharing  

#### F2F Requirements
* Created a Data science challenge and proceeded up to review
* Reviewer reviewed the submission and filled the scorecard
* Then the reviewer clicked Save for later button
* Expected: The score should not be submitted, but it is being submitted now
* Note: This issue is common to all review scorecards including screening score cards)

**Video/Screenshot**: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1-Epy6Ac2Lasi9NGzr8EDKZzEMnlKIt_m/view?usp=share_link 

#### What To Submit?
* Create new patch based on the latest reskin-or-only
* Do not change the space/unnecessary updates to all files
* Do not break existing functions

name: Reporting Application - API Development #1
description:
# Challenge Objectives
* The goal of the challenge is to implement REST API with JAVA and MySQL.
* The API will be integrated with React based frontend app in a later challenge.

# Project Background
We are going to build a web application that allows users to download diagnostic reports for a specific field.

The application is supposed to provide several features like:
* Dashboard where users can download diagnostic reports and make inquiries.
* Users can request online consulting services on this application.
* Administrative screens used by support.
* Send email notifications to users.

# Technology Stack
* JAVA（Amazon Corretto 17）
* REST
* Spring Boot 2.7
* MySQL 8
* Auth0

# Challenge Requirements
For this challenge, we are going to build the REST API following the provided API List and database model. Please refresh this page after registering to the challenge to view the API details at the bottom of this page.

# General Requirements
* The provided tech stack must be followed (including versions)
* Must cover all the endpoints in the scope (see the table of endpoints below)
* When implementing each API, follow the Base Code if possible.
* Code needs to be properly designed and structured for easy maintenance (for example: you may want to divide the endpoints into different groups / folders based on the data model)
* Code should be properly documented
* Unit Tests are required.
* The file structure should conform to the standard as much as possible.

# Submission
Submit a single zip file that contains the following:
* The source code. (Any error detected by Lint should be fixed before the submission.)
* A detailed README in markdown format on how to configure, run, and test your submission
* A postman collection that can be used to test the positive and negative cases of the API
* The swagger spec if anything got updated during the challenge
* The sql scripts used to populate the database and/or any master / test data

# API Details
All the following endpoints from swagger are in scope.
## users
* auth0 signup: POST /users/signup
* titan signup: POST /users/me
* get user me: GET /users/me
* put user me: PUT /users/me
* search users: POST /users/search

## admin
* get admin me: GET /admins

## report
* download cra report me: POST /reports/cra/me/latest
* download cra report: POST /reports/cra/me/{id}
* get cra reports: GET /reports/cra/me

## contact
* post contact: POST /contacts

name: Topcoder Work Manager - Universal Nav header / footer - Content Security Policy problem
description:
## Project overview

The Topcoder Work Manager application is how copilots launch challenges on the Topcoder Platform.  

### Source code

The source code for Work Manager is publicly available here:

https://github.com/topcoder-platform/work-manager

We will target the "develop" branch.

To deploy properly locally, please see here:  https://platform-ui.topcoder-dev.com/dev-center/getting-started

You need to specifically look at the `Hosts file update` and `Install the proxy and run it` sections to get login to work properly.  You must deploy and use `https://local.topcoder-dev.com` for proper local access.  If you attempt to run on `localhost`, you will not be able to login properly.

### Requirements

The full requirements for this F2F can be found here:

https://github.com/topcoder-platform/work-manager/issues/1462

### Submission

Your submission should be a git patch file against the "develop" branch commit hash db3b482f or later

Your submission must pass all existing tests and not introduce any failures or any changes to *existing* functionality.

name: Rapid Development Match (RDM) - Practice Round, January 2023
description:
## Overview
This is an old problem, related to TCO20. It is a great example of an RDM style problem. The only difference is that this practice round is *not* rated, and there is only one problem. Normal rated RDM rounds usually have three problems. A practice round is a great way to get started with RDM competitions without risking your rank and without the pressure of the live competition.

You can refer to [this](https://www.topcoder.com/thrive/articles/docker-quickstart-for-rdm-and-automated-testing-challenges) short article for a quick explanation on the submission format.

## Problem description

Bill is an adventurous explorer. One day, he came to a castle named TCO20. It is said that there is a precious treasure in this castle. The treasure is stored in a mysterious box. The only way to get to the treasure is to find the key to unlock the box. Bill looked for clues floor by floor. When he came to the 64th floor underground (Base 64), he found a paper with a bunch of strange symbols:

 `e0MgIjpUMDJleSIia095aXNfX3J1aGl7c3RvZXRfZWsifWVjX3JzeX0=`

Below the symbols there is a note: "13 is your lucky number. You will need to get the JSON value."

![p5r5tBCd3B](https://i.imgur.com/ULzTJfu.png)

**Hint:** Bill is clever, he quickly recognized the following patterns from the note.

- The strange symbols make a base64 encoded string.
- The final deciphered text should be a stringified JSON object.
- The format of the JSON object is `{"key": value}` because we need to decipher the treasure **key**.
- 13 is your lucky number. To decipher the key, maybe we can organize every 13 characters in a row and then rearrange the order of these characters?

## Requirements

**In this challenge, you need to write  web app to help Bill decipher the key from the symbols. We can't tell you the exact decipher steps. You will have to find it by yourself from the background story above and the hints below. **

### Requirements for the app

During the system testing, there will be many other base64 encoded strings that need to be deciphered in the same way, so your code should not just be dedicated to deciphering `e0MgIjpUMDJleSIia095aXNfX3J1aGl7c3RvZXRfZWsifWVjX3JzeX0=`.

# Hints

(1) First, you will need to decode the base64 string.

(2) Then extract the string inside `{}`, and follow the steps below.

(3) Split the message into separate rows. Each row should contain 13 characters. Affirming that when they write the algorithm later to process each row, they can be safely assumed that each row will indeed be of length 13.

(4) Unscramble the first line into human-readable words. That is, rearrange the characters in the first row such that they start making sense. Every character, including space, quotes, braces, etc are important. Do not discard any character. You are expected to only rearrange the characters. You will always rearrange the character within the same row. A character will never be moved to a different row.

(5) Apply for the same rearrangement order from the first line to the remaining lines. 

- Example: If, in the first row, the string is “dPresient”, and you figure out that you need to move the character d (at index 0) to index 5 (so that the “unscrambled” string says President), for ALL the other rows, a character at index 0 will always be moved to index 5.
- Thus, if you correctly figure out the arrangement of characters in the first row, you will automatically figure out the arrangement of the characters in the rest of the rows
Additionally, for EVERY input text, you will follow the same arrangement. That is, if you figure out the arrangement for the provided example, you can use the same rearrangement order for ALL other input and thus translate your understanding into an algorithm that can be easily implemented as code.
- Additionally, for EVERY input text, you will follow the same arrangement. That is, if you figure out the arrangement for the provided example, you can use the same rearrangement order for ALL other input and thus translate your understanding into an algorithm that can be easily implemented as code.

(6). After unscrambling the lines, it will be human-readable text.

(7). Concatenate all the lines into a single string. Enclose this string in `{` and `}` characters. Remember - the output is a JSON object. That will be the output.

### Requirements for the Web App
This page needs to be the index page of your web app and must contains these elements: 

1. Add textarea with id `input`. This textarea is the input where user fill the provided characters. 
2. Add button with text `Decipher` below textarea. Click this button needs to run the deciphering process and return the values to another textarea below
3. Add another textarea with id `output` below Decipher button. This textarea is where user see the deciphering result. 


It will help the automated-tester to validate your submission.

# Constraints & Guidelines

- Please use the forums to ask any questions.
- Minimal code quality is expected for the reviewer to review the submission and go through your code
- Obvious and deliberate code obfuscation will be rejected.
- Collaborating/Cheating in any way with anyone else (member or not) during a rated event is considered cheating.
- An excessive amount of unused content should be avoided.
- Code Readability is expected with necessary comments, indentation, and somewhat refactored code (as much as possible)

## Technology Stack

- Programming language: No limitation
- Docker

## Submission Deliverables

Submit a zip file. Inside the zip file, there needs to be a folder name code. Inside the code folder, you need to have a Dockerfile which we will use to build the image and start your application by starting a container using the image. The code folder can contain other folders and files but it needs to contain the Dockerfile. The zip file too can contain other folders and files, but it needs to have the code folder at the ROOT.

## Sample submissions

* Python/Flash [code_python.zip](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1U-CSRr008s9FmK4L6WOT3O5RMNQ_m12B/view?usp=share_link)
* ExpressJS [code_node.zip](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Ru6KCvUwX_VMQRNLpVzXHz7SUvCrKLxU/view?usp=share_link)
* Tester for testing your solution locally [sample-and-tester.zip](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1WoMDBOdIczHobG-uDsOWBAA4kJCzMCCY/view?usp=share_link)

name: Reporting Application - Thymeleaf Based HTML Email Templates
description:
# Challenge/Task Objectives
Build Thymeleaf-based HTML templates to be used in the emails the application sends.

# Project Background
We are going to build a web application that allows users to download diagnostic reports for a specific field.
The application is supposed to provide several features like;
* Dashboard where users can download diagnostic reports and make inquiries.
* Users can request online consulting services on this application.
* Administrative screens used by support.
* Send email notifications to users.

# Technology Stack
* HTML
* CSS
* Thymeleaf

# Challenge Requirements
For this challenge, we are going to build the HTML for the email that covers the following emails:
* R002: Account Create Completion
* R006: Contact Email (Support)
* R007: Contact Email (User)
* R010: Assessment Update Notification
* R022: Assessment Complete Notification
* R026: Assessment Complete Notification (Agency)

You can refer to the XD file of each email screen after registration.

# General Requirements
* Must develop the HTML email templates based on Thymeleaf
* HTML with responsive design for mobile mail app, client mail software and web client
* Follow HTML email development best practices as recommended in https://www.emailonacid.com/blog/article/email-development/email-development-best-practices-2/ and https://templates.mailchimp.com/getting-started/html-email-basics/
* The main content should have fluid width
* No errors with production build
* You are expected to create a detailed README file including information on how to setup, run and verify your application

# HTML Requirements
* HTML should be valid HTML5 compliant.
* Provide comments on the page elements to give a clear explanation of the code usage. The goal is to help future developers understand the code.
* Please use clean INDENTATION for all HTML code so future developers can follow the code.
* All HTML code naming should not have any conflicts or errors.
* Element and Attribute names should be in lowercase and use a "-" or camel naming to separate multiple-word classes (i.e.. "main-content", or "mainContent)
* Use semantically correct tags- use H tags for headers, etc. Use strong and em tags instead of bold and italic tags.
* Validate your code- reviewers may accept minor validation errors, but please comment your reason for any validation errors. Use the validators listed in the scorecard.

# Licenses & Attribution
Third-party assets used to build your item (likely not needed at all for this F2F) must be properly licensed and free for commercial use. MIT and Apache v2 licenses are ok, for any others please check with us to get approval first Sufficient information regarding third-party assets must be present in your documentation. This includes the author, license info and a direct link to the asset online.

# Submission
Submit a single zip file that contains all the Thymeleaf-based HTML email templates and a detailed README in markdown format.

name: Topcoder Work Manager - Fix submission phase display on 2 round challenge
description:
## Project overview

The Topcoder Work Manager application is how copilots launch challenges on the Topcoder Platform.  

### Source code

The source code for Work Manager is publicly available here:

https://github.com/topcoder-platform/work-manager

We will target the "develop" branch

### Requirements

The full requirements for this F2F can be found here:

https://github.com/topcoder-platform/work-manager/issues/1464

### Submission

Your submission should be a git patch file against the "develop" branch commit hash a90f1581 or later

Your submission must pass all existing tests and not introduce any failures or any changes to *existing* functionality.
